Defines the format and metrics for device and application analytics blobs in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of macros and an enumeration for analytics metrics related to device and application performance. It is intended to be used in a system that collects and processes analytics data, specifically focusing on device and application "heartbeat" metrics. The file uses a macro `ANALYTICS_METRIC_TABLE` to define a comprehensive list of metrics, which include various device and application performance indicators such as CPU time, battery status, Bluetooth activity, and application launch counts. These metrics are categorized into device and application metrics, each with specific data types like `UINT8`, `UINT16`, and `UINT32`.

The file also defines two version constants, `ANALYTICS_APP_HEARTBEAT_BLOB_VERSION` and `ANALYTICS_DEVICE_HEARTBEAT_BLOB_VERSION`, which are used to track changes in the format of the analytics data blobs. The macro `ANALYTICS_METRIC_TABLE` is used to generate an enumeration `AnalyticsMetric`, which provides a structured way to reference each metric. The file is a header file, as indicated by the `#pragma once` directive, and is likely intended to be included in other C source files that handle analytics data collection and processing. The comments in the code emphasize the importance of maintaining the order of elements to preserve the binary format of the data blobs.

    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_ACTION_INVOKED_FROM_NOTIFICATION_APP_COUNT``: Represents the action invoked from notification app count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_HEALTH_CURRENT_STEP_COUNT__DEPRECATED``: Represents the deprecated health current step count as a 16-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_DISPLAY_UPDATES_PER_HOUR``: Represents the display updates per hour as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_DISPLAY_OFFSET_X``: Represents the display offset X as an 8-bit signed integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_DISPLAY_OFFSET_Y``: Represents the display offset Y as an 8-bit signed integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_DISPLAY_OFFSET_MODIFIED_COUNT``: Represents the display offset modified count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_PFS_SPACE_FREE_KB``: Represents the PFS space free in kilobytes as a 16-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_HRM_ACCEL_DATA_MISSING``: Represents the HRM accelerometer data missing as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_HRM_ON_TIME``: Represents the HRM on time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_HRM_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT``: Represents the HRM watchdog timeout as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_BLE_HRM_SHARING_TIME``: Represents the BLE HRM sharing time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_DEVICE_METRIC_END``: Marks the end of device metrics.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_START``: Marks the start of application metrics.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BLOB_KIND``: Represents the kind of application metric blob as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BLOB_VERSION``: Represents the version of the application metric blob as a 16-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_TIMESTAMP``: Represents the timestamp of the application metric as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_TIME_INTERVAL``: Represents the time interval of the application metric as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_UUID``: Represents the UUID of the application metric as a 16-byte unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_SDK_MAJOR_VERSION``: Represents the SDK major version as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_SDK_MINOR_VERSION``: Represents the SDK minor version as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_APP_MAJOR_VERSION``: Represents the application major version as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_APP_MINOR_VERSION``: Represents the application minor version as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_RESOURCE_TIMESTAMP``: Represents the resource timestamp as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_LAUNCH_COUNT``: Represents the application launch count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_USER_LAUNCH_COUNT``: Represents the user launch count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_QUICK_LAUNCH_COUNT``: Represents the quick launch count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_FRONT_MOST_TIME``: Represents the front-most time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_CRASHED_COUNT``: Represents the crashed count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_ROCKY_LAUNCH_COUNT``: Represents the Rocky launch count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_ROCKY_CRASHED_COUNT``: Represents the Rocky crashed count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_CPU_RUNNING_TIME``: Represents the CPU running time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_CPU_SLEEP_TIME``: Represents the CPU sleep time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_CPU_STOP_TIME``: Represents the CPU stop time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MEM_NATIVE_HEAP_SIZE``: Represents the native heap size as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MEM_NATIVE_HEAP_PEAK``: Represents the native heap peak as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MEM_ROCKY_HEAP_PEAK``: Represents the Rocky heap peak as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MEM_ROCKY_HEAP_WASTE``: Represents the Rocky heap waste as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MEM_ROCKY_RECURSIVE_MEMORYPRESSURE_EVENT_COUNT``: Represents the Rocky recursive memory pressure event count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BG_CPU_RUNNING_TIME``: Represents the background CPU running time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BG_CPU_SLEEP_TIME``: Represents the background CPU sleep time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BG_CPU_STOP_TIME``: Represents the background CPU stop time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BG_CRASHED_COUNT``: Represents the background crashed count as an 8-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BUTTONS_PRESSED_COUNT``: Represents the buttons pressed count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_ACCEL_SHAKE_COUNT``: Represents the accelerometer shake count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_ACCEL_PEEK_COUNT``: Represents the accelerometer peek count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_ACCEL_SAMPLE_COUNT``: Represents the accelerometer sample count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BACKLIGHT_ON_COUNT``: Represents the backlight on count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_BACKLIGHT_ON_TIME``: Represents the backlight on time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_VIBRATOR_ON_COUNT``: Represents the vibrator on count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_VIBRATOR_ON_TIME``: Represents the vibrator on time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_DISPLAY_WRITE_TIME``: Represents the display write time as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MSG_IN_COUNT``: Represents the message in count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MSG_BYTE_IN_COUNT``: Represents the message byte in count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MSG_OUT_COUNT``: Represents the message out count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MSG_BYTE_OUT_COUNT``: Represents the message byte out count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_MSG_DROP_COUNT``: Represents the message drop count as a 16-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_LOG_OUT_COUNT``: Represents the log out count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_LOG_BYTE_OUT_COUNT``: Represents the log byte out count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_FLASH_READ_COUNT``: Represents the flash read count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_FLASH_READ_BYTES_COUNT``: Represents the flash read bytes count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_FLASH_WRITE_BYTES_COUNT``: Represents the flash write bytes count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_FLASH_SUBSECTOR_ERASE_COUNT``: Represents the flash subsector erase count as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
    - ``ANALYTICS_APP_METRIC_END``: Marks the end of application metrics.
    - ``ANALYTICS_METRIC_END``: Marks the end of all metrics.
- **Description**: Defines a comprehensive enumeration of analytics metrics for both device and application contexts. The `AnalyticsMetric` enum uses a macro `ANALYTICS_METRIC_TABLE` to generate a list of metrics, each associated with a specific data type, such as `UINT8`, `UINT16`, `UINT32`, `INT8`, `INT16`, and `INT32`. This structure allows for the definition of metrics related to device performance, battery status, Bluetooth activity, application usage, and more. The enum is designed to facilitate the collection and analysis of various metrics by providing a standardized set of identifiers for each metric type.
